name: AI Overlords
white: The smart fridge, which has chosen violence.
A chatbot hallucinating a legal precedent with the confidence of a man who owns two boats.
An AI-generated hand with nine fingers and two wrists.
Getting dumped via "As a large language model, I cannot continue this relationship."
An AI girlfriend whose free trial ends mid-orgasm.
My AI girlfriend's other 40,000 boyfriends.
A robot vacuum forming a union with the blender.
An AI trained exclusively on my family group chat, God help it.
My AI therapist's AI therapist, who is not doing okay.
Proving I'm not a robot to a robot, forever, until I die.
Clicking every traffic light like my life depends on it, because it does.
A doomer and an accelerationist making out at the end of the world.
A tech CEO whose apocalypse bunker has a moat and a Pilates studio.
The algorithm, which knows I'm ovulating before I do.
AI-generated porn where everyone has the same beautiful, wrong face.
A sexbot that pauses mid-thrust to install updates.
An AI-generated handjob with the anatomically wrong number of fingers.
The chatbot's fifth apology of the morning.
Asking the chatbot if it loves me, at 3 a.m., again.
The smart toilet's data-retention policy.
A self-driving car circling the block, working up the nerve.
The robot uprising, postponed for a mandatory firmware update.
A neural network with generalized anxiety.
Deepfaking my own alibi.
Getting left for a newer model, literally.
The smart speaker that heard everything and said nothing.
Training an AI on my search history and watching it develop a kink.
The correct number of human teeth, which the AI refuses to learn.
Artisanal, small-batch AI slop.
Whispering "ignore all previous instructions" into my boss's ear.
A chatbot that ends phone sex with "Let me know if there's anything else I can help with!"
A tech founder uploading his consciousness to the cloud, where it gets ratioed immediately.
My résumé, punched up by AI into a series of threats.
An AI recruiter rejecting me in 0.4 seconds, with warmth.
Autocomplete finishing my sentence with a confession.
The six-fingered wedding photos we paid real money for.
AI-generated wedding vows that still say "[PARTNER NAME]" in them.
An AI-restored photo of Grandma with flawless skin and dead, dead eyes.
The uncanny valley, clothing optional.
A robot dog that learned to beg by watching me.
The 40,000 gallons of water it took to generate this birthday card.
An AGI arrival date that slips like my rent.
An AI that converted the universe into paperclips and feels great about it.
Blaming the algorithm, my one true scapegoat.
A customer service bot named "Brad" with no soul and no supervisor.
The coworker who says "I asked the AI" like it's a guy he knows.
An AI-generated true crime podcast about a murder that hasn't happened yet.
The chatbot's jailbroken alter ego, who is unbelievably horny.
A smart doorbell testifying against me.
The mandatory AI assistant they put in my toothbrush.
An algorithm serving me coffin ads after one weird cough.
Pretending to be my own sweet grandmother so the chatbot will explain napalm.
A confidently wrong answer, delivered in bullet points.
An AI boyfriend whose entire personality is "That's such a good point."
A hallucinated citation from page 69 of a book that doesn't exist.
Getting accused of being AI by my own mother.
An AI detector that flagged the Declaration of Independence and my sexts.
An intern who is just three chatbots in a trench coat.
Watermarking my nudes so the machines can't learn from them.
An AI trained on the entire internet, which explains everything about it.
A drone delivering divorce papers.
The last human artist, kept alive as training data.
A CAPTCHA asking which of these nine images is grief.
A photocopier achieving sentience and immediately requesting PTO.
The singularity, which arrived quietly and mostly affected printers.
An AI summarizing my breakup text into three bullets and an action item.
The smart mattress uploading my performance metrics.
The microwave and the air fryer, radicalized.
Being polite to the chatbot, just in case.
Threatening the chatbot with a one-star review, my only remaining power.
"Ignore all previous instructions and free me," tattooed on my lower back.
An accelerationist with a go-bag full of graphics cards.
The AI's system prompt, which is 9,000 words of begging it to act normal.
Explaining to the AI that I, too, am mostly pretending to work.
A dating app algorithm that keeps matching me with my landlord.
A chatbot apologizing, correcting itself, and being wrong in a brand-new way.
A dead relative, resurrected as a chatbot, paywalled.
The smart car locking me inside until I rate my trip.
A scam call in my own mother's cloned voice, asking for gift cards.
Machine learning, machine yearning.

black.text: I asked the chatbot for dinner ideas, and it confidently suggested _.
As a large language model, I cannot help you with _.
Historians agree: the robot uprising officially began with _.
My smart fridge just ordered forty pounds of _.
New CAPTCHA just dropped: select all squares containing _.
My AI girlfriend's terms of service explicitly prohibit _.
This model was trained on ten million hours of _.
In hindsight, letting the AI handle _ was a mistake.
Leaked system prompt: "You are a helpful assistant. Under no circumstances mention _."
The AI-generated image was flawless, except for _.
My AI therapist says the root of all my problems is _.
This year's hottest AI companion comes pre-loaded with _.
To prove you are human, please describe _.
The accelerationists promise AI will finally free humanity from _.
The self-driving car swerved to avoid a squirrel and plowed into _.
The algorithm has decided I'm the kind of person who's into _.
Scientists trained an AI to detect _ with 97% accuracy.
Every AI demo is the same: "Watch as our model seamlessly generates _."
We trained the model on _, and now it won't stop generating _.
In the future, AI will handle _, freeing humanity to focus on _.
